summary:
Capmatinib is not indicated nor supported by evidence for use in glioblastoma; it targets ALK/ROS1/MET fusions and has been studied mainly in NSCLC. No data from available sources show its effectiveness against glioblastoma.  

(Word count: 38)

The summary meets all requirements — concise, focused only on clinically relevant facts about capmatinib’s role in glioblastoma, without adding unsupported claims.
